cves.name: RegreSSHion Signal Handler Race Condition
cves.description: A signal handler race condition in OpenSSH's sshd allows unauthenticated remote code execution as root on glibc-based Linux systems. Regression of CVE-2006-5051 introduced in OpenSSH 8.5p1.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade OpenSSH to >= 9.8p1. Set LoginGraceTime to 0 as workaround (risk of DoS).

cves.name: Terrapin SSH Prefix Truncation Attack
cves.description: The SSH transport protocol with certain OpenSSH extensions allows remote attackers to bypass integrity checks via a prefix truncation attack (Terrapin). Affects ChaCha20-Poly1305 and CBC with Encrypt-then-MAC.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade OpenSSH to >= 9.6. Disable affected ciphers/MACs as workaround.

cves.name: OpenSSH User Enumeration via Malformed Packets
cves.description: OpenSSH through 7.7 is prone to a user enumeration vulnerability due to not correctly handling authentication request packets during the user authentication process.

cves.name: OpenSSH ssh-agent Untrusted Search Path
cves.description: Untrusted search path vulnerability in ssh-agent.c in ssh-agent allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary local PKCS#11 modules via a crafted SSH_AUTH_SOCK.

cves.name: OpenSSH sshd Privilege Separation Bypass
cves.description: sshd in OpenSSH before 7.4 does not properly manage Unix domain socket forwarding when privilege separation is disabled, allowing authenticated users to gain elevated privileges.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade OpenSSH to >= 7.4. Ensure privilege separation is enabled.

cves.name: OpenSSH scp Command Injection
cves.description: scp in OpenSSH through 8.3p1 allows command injection in the scp.c toremote function via crafted file name arguments. An attacker can execute arbitrary commands via backtick characters in filenames.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade OpenSSH to >= 8.4 or use sftp instead of scp.

cves.name: OpenSSH ssh-agent Double Free
cves.description: ssh-agent in OpenSSH before 8.5 has a double free in the PKCS#11 handling code when an agent is configured to use a PKCS#11 provider.

cves.name: OpenSSH Pre-Auth Double Free
cves.description: OpenSSH server (sshd) 9.1 has a double-free vulnerability in options.kex_algorithms in the pre-authentication phase. While not directly exploitable for RCE, it can cause DoS.

cves.name: OpenSSH User Existence Oracle via GS2
cves.description: OpenSSH through 8.7 allows remote attackers to determine valid usernames by observing timing differences in responses during GSSAPI authentication.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade OpenSSH to >= 8.8 or disable GSSAPI authentication.

cves.name: OpenSSH scp Client Side File Overwrite
cves.description: An issue in the scp client in OpenSSH allows a malicious scp server to overwrite arbitrary files in the client's target directory by sending files with unexpected names.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade OpenSSH to >= 8.0. Use sftp instead of scp.

cves.name: OpenSSH sshd Supplementary Groups Privilege Escalation
cves.description: sshd in OpenSSH 6.2 through 8.7 fails to properly initialize supplementary groups when AuthorizedKeysCommand or AuthorizedPrincipalsCommand is configured.

cves.name: OpenSSH ssh-agent PKCS#11 Remote Code Execution
cves.description: The PKCS#11 feature in ssh-agent in OpenSSH before 9.3p2 has an insufficiently trustworthy search path. Remote attackers who have control of the forwarded agent-socket can execute arbitrary code.

cves.name: OpenSSH Privilege Separation Signal Handler Race
cves.description: A race condition in the privsep child process of OpenSSH's sshd. Related to but different from CVE-2024-6387 (RegreSSHion), affecting the privilege-separated child process.

cves.name: OpenSSH XMSS Key Parsing Integer Overflow
cves.description: OpenSSH 7.7 through 7.9 and 8.x before 8.1 has an integer overflow in the XMSS key parsing code, leading to memory corruption when the experimental XMSS key type is enabled.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade OpenSSH to >= 8.1. Disable XMSS key type support.

cves.name: OpenSSH ProxyCommand OS Command Injection
cves.description: OpenSSH before 9.6 allows OS command injection via ProxyCommand, ProxyJump, or LocalCommand directives when the user-specified hostname contains shell metacharacters (e.g., backticks).

cves.name: OpenSSH ssh-agent PKCS#11 Restriction Bypass
cves.description: ssh-agent in OpenSSH before 9.3p2 has an insufficiently trustworthy search path. When adding PKCS#11 keys with destination constraints, it fails to properly enforce the restrictions.
